Story: Add real-time notification bell to the header
Context files: src/components/Header.tsx, src/services/NotificationService.ts, src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts

{"description":"

Summary

Adds a real-time notification bell icon to the application header that displays unread count and a dropdown list of recent notifications.

Problem

Users have no way to see system notifications without navigating to a separate page, leading to missed alerts about PR reviews, build completions, and story assignments.

Solution

Add a bell icon component to src/components/Header.tsx that subscribes to WebSocket events via useWebSocket hook and displays a badge with unread count plus a dropdown panel.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the user is logged in, when a new notification arrives via WebSocket, then the bell badge count increments within 1 second without page refresh.
  • Given the bell shows unread count > 0, when the user clicks the bell, then a dropdown appears showing the 10 most recent notifications sorted by timestamp descending.
  • Given the dropdown is open, when the user clicks a notification, then it navigates to the relevant resource and marks that notification as read.
  • Given all notifications are read, when viewing the header, then no badge number is displayed on the bell icon.
","frontend":"
  • src/components/Header.tsx - add NotificationBell component slot
  • src/components/NotificationBell.tsx - new component with bell icon, badge, and dropdown
  • src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts - add notification event subscription
","backend":"
  • src/services/NotificationService.ts - add getRecent(limit) and markRead(id) methods
","qa":"
  • Trigger a notification event - verify bell badge increments in real-time
  • Click bell with 3 unread - verify dropdown shows all 3 with timestamps
  • Click a notification - verify navigation occurs and count decrements
  • Mark all as read - verify badge disappears
"}

Story: Implement dark mode toggle in settings
Context files: src/app/settings/SettingsPanel.tsx, src/services/ThemeService.ts, src/styles/variables.css

{"description":"

Summary

Adds a dark mode toggle to the settings panel allowing users to switch between light and dark themes.

Problem

Users working in low-light environments experience eye strain from the always-light interface with no option to reduce brightness.

Solution

Add a toggle switch in SettingsPanel.tsx that calls ThemeService.setTheme() and persists preference to localStorage, applying CSS custom property overrides from variables.css.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the settings panel is open, when the user toggles dark mode on, then all page backgrounds change to the dark palette (#1a1a2e) within 200ms.
  • Given dark mode is active, when the user refreshes the browser, then dark mode persists via localStorage.
  • Given dark mode is active, when the user toggles it off, then the interface reverts to the light theme immediately.
  • Given the system prefers dark mode (prefers-color-scheme: dark), when the user has no saved preference, then dark mode is applied by default.
","frontend":"
  • src/app/settings/SettingsPanel.tsx - add dark mode toggle with label and Switch component
  • src/services/ThemeService.ts - add setTheme(mode: 'light'|'dark') and getTheme() methods
  • src/styles/variables.css - add :root[data-theme='dark'] custom property overrides
","backend":null,"qa":"
  • Toggle dark mode on - verify all backgrounds use dark palette colors
  • Refresh page in dark mode - verify theme persists across sessions
  • Toggle dark mode off - verify complete revert to light theme
  • Clear localStorage and set OS to dark mode - verify auto-detection works
"}

Story: Add pagination to the story backlog list
Context files: src/components/BacklogList.tsx, src/server/routes/stories.ts, src/hooks/usePagination.ts

{"description":"

Summary

Adds server-side pagination to the story backlog list to improve performance when displaying large numbers of stories.

Problem

The backlog list currently loads all stories at once, causing slow initial render (3-5 seconds) and excessive memory usage when backlogs exceed 200 items.

Solution

Implement cursor-based pagination in the stories API route and connect it to a usePagination hook in BacklogList.tsx with page size of 25 items and infinite scroll loading.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given a backlog with 100+ stories, when the page loads, then only the first 25 stories render and the API responds within 200ms.
  • Given the user scrolls to the bottom of the list, when the scroll sentinel becomes visible, then the next 25 stories load automatically without a full page reload.
  • Given all stories are loaded, when scrolling to the bottom, then no further API calls are made and a 'No more stories' indicator appears.
  • Given the user applies a filter, when the filter changes, then pagination resets to page 1 with filtered results.
","frontend":"
  • src/components/BacklogList.tsx - replace full list render with paginated IntersectionObserver pattern
  • src/hooks/usePagination.ts - implement cursor-based pagination hook with loading states
","backend":"
  • src/server/routes/stories.ts - add cursor and limit query params, return nextCursor in response
","qa":"
  • Load backlog with 150 stories - verify only 25 render initially
  • Scroll to bottom 3 times - verify 75 total stories displayed progressively
  • Apply category filter - verify list resets and shows filtered page 1
  • Reach end of list - verify no infinite loading loop occurs
"}

Story: Add file upload support to the comment thread
Context files: src/components/CommentThread.tsx, src/server/routes/comments.ts, src/server/storage.ts

{"description":"

Summary

Enables file attachments (images, PDFs, documents) in comment threads on stories and tasks.

Problem

Team members cannot share screenshots, design mockups, or documents directly in comment threads, forcing them to use external links or separate communication channels.

Solution

Add a file upload button to CommentThread.tsx that uploads via multipart form to the comments route, stores files via storage.ts (local disk or blob storage), and renders inline previews for images.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the comment input is focused, when the user clicks the attachment icon, then a file picker opens accepting images (png, jpg, gif), PDFs, and documents (doc, docx) up to 10MB.
  • Given a file is selected, when the upload completes, then a thumbnail preview appears in the comment draft area before submission.
  • Given a comment with an image attachment is posted, when viewing the thread, then the image renders inline at max-width 400px with a click-to-expand overlay.
  • Given a non-image attachment, when viewing the thread, then a download link with filename and size is displayed.
","frontend":"
  • src/components/CommentThread.tsx - add attachment button, upload progress indicator, inline preview
  • src/components/FilePreview.tsx - new component for rendering image thumbnails and file download links
","backend":"
  • src/server/routes/comments.ts - add multipart upload handler, validate file type and size
  • src/server/storage.ts - add saveFile(buffer, metadata) and getFileUrl(id) methods
","qa":"
  • Upload a 5MB PNG - verify thumbnail appears and image renders inline after posting
  • Upload an 11MB file - verify rejection with clear error message
  • Upload a PDF - verify download link displays with correct filename
  • Upload .exe file - verify rejection (not in allowed types)
"}

Story: Implement keyboard shortcuts for common actions
Context files: src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts, src/components/ShortcutOverlay.tsx, src/app/App.tsx

{"description":"

Summary

Adds keyboard shortcuts for frequently-used actions (new story, search, navigation) with a discoverable shortcut overlay.

Problem

Power users must click through multiple UI elements for common actions, slowing their workflow. There is no keyboard-driven navigation available.

Solution

Implement a useKeyboardShortcuts hook registered at App.tsx level that listens for Ctrl/Cmd+key combinations and triggers actions. Add a ShortcutOverlay component (triggered by ?) that displays all available shortcuts.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the user presses Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac), when on any page, then the global search modal opens with focus in the search input.
  • Given the user presses Ctrl+N, when on the backlog page, then the new story creation form opens.
  • Given the user presses ?, when not focused in a text input, then the keyboard shortcut overlay appears listing all shortcuts grouped by category.
  • Given a text input is focused, when the user presses shortcut keys, then they type normally and no shortcut fires.
","frontend":"
  • src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts - implement global keydown listener with shortcut registry and input-focus detection
  • src/components/ShortcutOverlay.tsx - modal overlay listing all shortcuts grouped by Navigation, Creation, and General
  • src/app/App.tsx - register useKeyboardShortcuts at root level
","backend":null,"qa":"
  • Press Ctrl+K on backlog page - verify search modal opens
  • Press Ctrl+N on backlog page - verify new story form opens
  • Press ? while not in input - verify overlay appears with all shortcuts
  • Focus a text input and press Ctrl+K - verify character is typed, not search opened
"}

You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Critical
- **SQL Injection vulnerability**: String interpolation in SQL query allows arbitrary SQL execution. Use parameterized queries: `db.query('INSERT INTO users (email, password, name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)', [email, hashedPassword, name])`
- **Plaintext password storage**: Password is stored directly without hashing. Use bcrypt or argon2: `const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12)`

### Warning
- **No input validation**: email, password, and name are not validated before use. Add schema validation (Zod, Joi) to reject malformed input early.
- **No duplicate check**: Inserting a duplicate email will throw an unhandled database error. Check existence first or handle the unique constraint error gracefully.

### Suggestion
- Add rate limiting to prevent brute-force account creation.
- Return 201 status code for resource creation instead of default 200.
- Wrap in try/catch to return structured error responses instead of crashing.
You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Warning
- **Memory leak risk**: Each cache entry spawns a setTimeout. Under high cardinality (many unique keys), this creates thousands of pending timers. Consider a periodic sweep or LRU eviction strategy instead.
- **No stale-while-revalidate**: Concurrent calls for the same uncached key will all trigger fetchFn simultaneously (thundering herd). Add an in-flight promise map to deduplicate.
- **Type safety**: `Map<string, any>` loses type information. The return type is correct but the internal storage could hold mismatched types between reads.

### Suggestion
- Add a `maxSize` option to bound memory usage and prevent unbounded growth.
- Consider storing the expiry timestamp alongside the value and checking on read (lazy expiration) rather than relying solely on setTimeout.
- Add a `cache.clear()` or `invalidate(key)` export for manual cache busting during tests or deployments.
You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Warning
- **No error isolation**: If any handler throws, `forEach` stops and subsequent handlers for the same event are skipped. Wrap each handler call in try/catch.
- **Memory leak**: No `removeAllListeners()` or `destroy()` method. Long-lived EventBus instances accumulate handlers if consumers forget to call `off()`.
- **Loose typing**: `Function` type provides no type safety on handler arguments. Use a generic event map pattern: `EventBus<Events extends Record<string, (...args: any[]) => void>>`.

### Suggestion
- Add a `once(event, handler)` method that auto-removes after first invocation - common pattern for connection/initialization events.
- Consider emitting asynchronously (queueMicrotask or nextTick) to prevent synchronous call stack issues with deeply nested emits.
- Add a maxListeners warning (similar to Node.js EventEmitter) to detect likely leaks during development.
You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Critical
- **Path traversal vulnerability**: The `env` parameter is user-controllable and directly interpolated into a file path. An attacker could pass `../../etc/passwd` to read arbitrary files. Validate env against an allowlist: `if (!['development','staging','production'].includes(env)) throw new Error('Invalid env')`

### Warning
- **No error handling**: `readFileSync` throws if the file doesn't exist, and `JSON.parse` throws on malformed JSON. Both crash the process with an unhelpful stack trace. Add try/catch with descriptive error messages.
- **Secrets in config files**: `apiKey` in a JSON file is likely committed to version control. Use environment variables for secrets instead.
- **Synchronous file read**: Blocks the event loop at startup. Acceptable for init but document that this should only be called once at boot.

### Suggestion
- Add schema validation (Zod) to the parsed config to catch missing/malformed fields at startup rather than at first use.
- Use `resolve(process.cwd(), 'config', ...)` instead of relative path for predictable resolution regardless of working directory.

Story: Integrate Ollama health check into the dashboard status panel
Context files: src/components/Dashboard.tsx, src/components/StatusPanel.tsx, src/server/routes/ollama.ts

{"description":"

Summary

Adds real-time Ollama model health status to the dashboard's status panel, showing model availability, last pull time, and RAG readiness.

Problem

Users cannot see whether the local AI model is online, which model is active, or whether the RAG index is ready without checking the terminal or API manually.

Solution

Poll /api/ollama/health every 30 seconds from StatusPanel.tsx and display a health card with online/offline indicator, active model name, last pulled timestamp, and RAG status.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the dashboard loads, when Ollama is running, then the status panel shows a green 'Online' badge with the active model name.
  • Given Ollama is not running, when the health check fails, then the panel shows a red 'Offline' badge with 'Cloud fallback active' text.
  • Given the RAG index is built, when viewing the status panel, then a 'RAG Ready' indicator appears in green.
  • Given a model was pulled within the last hour, when viewing status, then the 'Last Updated' timestamp displays as relative time (e.g. '5 minutes ago').
","frontend":"
  • src/components/StatusPanel.tsx - add OllamaHealthCard sub-component with polling interval
  • src/components/Dashboard.tsx - include StatusPanel in the dashboard grid layout
","backend":"
  • src/server/routes/ollama.ts - already exposes /api/ollama/health (no changes needed)
","qa":"
  • Start Ollama and load dashboard - verify green Online badge appears with model name
  • Stop Ollama and wait 30s - verify badge changes to red Offline
  • Run reindex and refresh - verify RAG Ready indicator turns green
"}

Story: Add token usage tracking chart to the agent dashboard
Context files: src/components/AgentDashboard.tsx, src/server/routes/tokens.ts, src/components/charts/TokenChart.tsx

{"description":"

Summary

Displays a stacked bar chart showing cloud vs Ollama token usage per agent over the current session, updated in real-time.

Problem

There is no visibility into how many tokens each agent consumes or what percentage comes from the local model vs cloud API, making cost optimization difficult.

Solution

Create a TokenChart component using the existing token ledger data from /api/tokens, rendered as a stacked horizontal bar chart per agent with cloud (blue) and Ollama (green) segments.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given the dashboard is open, when token data exists, then a stacked bar chart displays with one bar per agent showing cloud and Ollama token counts.
  • Given an agent makes an LLM call, when the token ledger updates, then the chart reflects the new totals within 10 seconds.
  • Given hovering over a bar segment, when the tooltip appears, then it shows exact token counts and percentage split.
  • Given no token data exists (fresh session), when viewing the chart, then a placeholder message 'No token usage recorded yet' appears.
","frontend":"
  • src/components/charts/TokenChart.tsx - implement stacked horizontal bar chart with cloud/ollama segments
  • src/components/AgentDashboard.tsx - add TokenChart to the metrics section with polling
","backend":"
  • src/server/routes/tokens.ts - ensure GET /api/tokens returns per-agent breakdown (already exists)
","qa":"
  • Trigger Ollama generation and cloud call - verify both segments appear in chart
  • Hover over chart segment - verify tooltip shows exact numbers
  • Fresh session with no calls - verify empty state message
"}

You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Critical
- **Synchronous execution blocks event loop**: `execSync` blocks the entire process during build. If multiple files change rapidly (common during git checkout or save-all), they queue up and execute sequentially, potentially taking minutes. Use `exec` or `spawn` with debouncing.

### Warning
- **No debouncing**: File watchers fire per-file. A single save-all operation on 20 files triggers 20 sequential builds. Add a debounce (300-500ms) to batch rapid changes into a single build.
- **No error handling**: If `npm run build` fails, `execSync` throws and crashes the watcher process entirely. Wrap in try/catch or use async exec with error callback.
- **Watching too broadly**: `'./src'` with no `ignored` option will trigger on temp files, .map files, and editor swap files. Add `ignored: /(^|[\/\\])\.|node_modules/`.

### Suggestion
- Extract the build trigger into a debounced function shared between change and add handlers to avoid duplication.
- Add a `ready` event handler to skip the initial add events when the watcher first scans the directory.
- Consider using `--watch` mode of the build tool instead of manual file watching if available.

Story: Add agent activity timeline to the Teams channel integration
Context files: src/server/routes/teams.ts, src/server/notifications.ts, src/components/ActivityTimeline.tsx

{"description":"

Solution

Extend the Teams notification system in notifications.ts to post Adaptive Cards containing the last 5 agent events whenever a significant phase change occurs (PR created, build complete, story done).

","acceptanceCriteria":"

  • Given the card is posted, when viewing it in Teams, then it shows agent name, story number, current phase, and the 5 most recent events with timestamps.
  • Given multiple agents are active, when cards are posted, then each card clearly identifies which agent sent it via a colored header.
  • Given the Teams webhook is unavailable, when a notification fails, then the error is logged but does not crash the server or block agent progress.
","frontend":"

","backend":"
  • src/server/notifications.ts - add buildActivityCard(agentId, events, phase) that generates Adaptive Card JSON
  • src/server/routes/teams.ts - add POST /api/teams/activity endpoint for manual trigger (testing)
","qa":"

  • Verify card shows correct agent name, story, and 5 events
  • Disable webhook URL - verify server continues without crashing
"}

Story: Add drag-and-drop story reordering in the sprint board
Context files: src/components/SprintBoard.tsx, src/components/StoryCard.tsx, src/server/routes/stories.ts

{"description":"

Summary

Enables drag-and-drop reordering of stories within sprint board columns (To Do, In Progress, Done) with server-side persistence of the new order.

Problem

Sprint board stories display in a fixed order. Team members cannot visually prioritize by dragging high-priority items to the top of each column.

Solution

Add drag-and-drop capability to SprintBoard.tsx using @dnd-kit/core for accessible drag interactions. Persist new order via PATCH /api/stories/reorder endpoint.

","acceptanceCriteria":"
  • Given a story card in the 'To Do' column, when the user drags it above another card, then the card visually reorders and a PATCH request persists the new position.
  • Given a story is dragged between columns, when dropped in 'In Progress', then its status updates to 'In Progress' and it appears at the drop position.
  • Given the drag is cancelled (Escape key or drop outside), when released, then the card returns to its original position with no API call made.
  • Given two users are viewing the same board, when one reorders, then the other sees the update on next refresh (optimistic UI for the actor).
","frontend":"

  • src/components/StoryCard.tsx - wrap in useSortable hook, add drag handle and drop animation
","backend":"

","qa":"

  • Drag story from To Do to In Progress - verify status changes and position is correct
  • Press Escape during drag - verify card returns to original spot
  • Drag with keyboard (Tab + Space + Arrow) - verify accessible reordering works
"}

You are a code reviewer. Provide a concise PR review summary with findings categorized as: Critical, Warning, Suggestion. Focus on bugs, security, performance, and maintainability.

## PR Review Summary

### Critical
- **Unhandled exception crashes server**: `jwt.verify()` throws on invalid/expired tokens (JsonWebTokenError, TokenExpiredError). Without try/catch, any invalid token crashes the request handler and potentially the process. Wrap in try/catch and return 401 on verification failure.

### Warning
- **Missing JWT_SECRET validation**: If `process.env.JWT_SECRET` is undefined, jwt.verify uses empty string as secret, making ALL tokens verifiable with an empty-string-signed JWT. Validate at startup: `if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET required')`
- **No token type validation**: The decoded payload is assigned directly to `req.user` without checking token type (access vs refresh) or required claims. A refresh token could be used as an access token.
- **No TypeScript types**: `req`, `res`, `next` are untyped. `req.user` assignment has no interface, leading to downstream type safety issues.

### Suggestion
- Add token expiration check explicitly or configure `jwt.verify` with `{ maxAge }` option for defense in depth.
- Consider extracting the secret to a config module that validates all required env vars at startup.
- Add request ID or user context to error responses for debugging production auth failures.
You are a senior frontend engineer. Return only code, no explanations unless asked.
Write a TypeScript class that manages a queue of async tasks with configurable concurrency limit. Support add(task), onDrain callback, and pause/resume.
